product_summary.one_sentence_summary: Premium doctor-specific OET Medicine prep for clinicians in the UAE and GCC who need a stronger pathway toward ECFMG or other international recognition routes.
product_summary.target_user: Doctors in the UAE/GCC, especially specialists and consultants, who want exam prep tailored to physician-level medical English.
product_summary.core_pain: Doctors need high-quality, route-relevant English preparation and feedback that matches their professional communication tasks.
product_summary.claimed_outcome: Better OET Medicine performance and a more confident path to international recognition or career advancement.

primary_buyer_type.reason: The offer is sold as 1:1 or small-group B2C exam prep.

secondary_buyer_types.reason: Some doctors may be employer-sponsored if a hospital wants them to clear an English pathway.

budget_owner.reason: The learner may pay personally, but employer sponsorship is plausible for senior clinicians.

buyer_influencers.reason: ECFMG and destination-country requirements motivate the purchase.
Hospital or department leaders can influence the study timeline or sponsorship decision.

externally_checkable_claims.claim_text: OET Medicine is the approved English language and communications skills test for International Medical Graduates applying for ECFMG certification.
externally_checkable_claims.notes: OET's official ECFMG page states this directly.

externally_checkable_claims.claim_text: OET Medicine is designed specifically for physicians and is an essential step in ECFMG's Pathways program.
externally_checkable_claims.notes: The official ECFMG page says OET Medicine is designed specifically for physicians and is an essential step in the Pathways program.

externally_checkable_claims.claim_text: OET is accepted by healthcare regulators in the UAE and recognized by healthcare regulators, educational institutions and visa and immigration services worldwide.
externally_checkable_claims.notes: The official OET site supports broad acceptance, and the UAE country page shows acceptance in Abu Dhabi and Dubai contexts.

externally_checkable_claims.claim_text: ECFMG now requires OET only and has dropped IELTS.
externally_checkable_claims.notes: The reviewed official sources confirm OET Medicine is approved and essential for Pathways, but they do not establish the broader 'OET only' framing.

externally_checkable_claims.claim_text: The curriculum includes 24 corrected medical letters and uses doctor-exclusive cohorts only.
externally_checkable_claims.notes: These are internal course-design choices and were not externally verified.

internal_claims_unverified.claim_text: ECFMG now requires OET only and has dropped IELTS.
internal_claims_unverified.reason_unverified: The official sources reviewed do not prove the broader exclusion claim.

internal_claims_unverified.claim_text: The curriculum includes 24 corrected medical letters and uses doctor-exclusive cohorts only.
internal_claims_unverified.reason_unverified: These are internal design choices, not public facts.

internal_claims_unverified.claim_text: A premium 1:1 format is necessary for doctors.
internal_claims_unverified.reason_unverified: No public source reviewed proves that this format is required rather than merely preferred.

internal_claims_unverified.claim_text: The tutor network is fully MD/MBBS + OET certified and sufficient for the target market.
internal_claims_unverified.reason_unverified: Tutor qualifications and adequacy were not externally verified.

regulator_entities.name: Educational Commission for Foreign Medical Graduates (ECFMG)
regulator_entities.relevance: The official ECFMG page explicitly uses OET Medicine for IMG certification pathways.

regulator_entities.name: OET
regulator_entities.relevance: The exam provider and preparation ecosystem are central to this product.

regulator_entities.name: Department of Health - Abu Dhabi
regulator_entities.relevance: The UAE page shows OET acceptance in Abu Dhabi and Dubai contexts.

framework_entities.name: ECFMG Pathways Program
framework_entities.relevance: The pathway framework for IMGs that the official OET Medicine page references.

framework_entities.name: OET Test
framework_entities.relevance: The test itself is the central external framework for this prep product.

regulatory_requirements.requirement: OET Medicine is the approved English language and communications skills test for International Medical Graduates applying for ECFMG certification.
regulatory_requirements.applies_to: International medical graduates pursuing ECFMG certification

regulatory_requirements.requirement: OET Medicine is an essential step in ECFMG's Pathways program.
regulatory_requirements.applies_to: International medical graduates applying through ECFMG Pathways

regulatory_requirements.requirement: OET is accepted by healthcare regulators in the UAE and by healthcare regulators, educational institutions and visa and immigration services worldwide.

competitors_or_substitutes.name: Intro to OET Medicine course for doctors
competitors_or_substitutes.notes: Official free OET introduction course competes directly with paid generic prep.

competitors_or_substitutes.name: OET free sample tests
competitors_or_substitutes.notes: Official sample tests reduce willingness to pay for generic prep and set the self-study baseline.

legal_claim_severity.reason: The offer is tied to ECFMG certification and other regulated international medical pathways.

authority_needed_for_delivery.reason: The course can be delivered without formal approval, but credible physician-focused instruction requires strong subject-matter expertise.

authority_needed_for_claims.reason: Claims about ECFMG usefulness and route relevance should be supported by expert review and current source checks.

proof_stack.reason: Doctors must see enough value to pay for a premium format instead of using free resources.
The main proof is improved OET Medicine scores or exam success.
Because the offer references ECFMG and route acceptance, partner or route proof matters if the claim language gets stronger.

proof_burden.reason: The product claims regulated-route relevance, so proof must reach beyond simple learning progress.

dependency_types.reason: Physician-level tutoring and route guidance need credible medical expertise.
The offer depends on MD/MBBS-qualified or equivalent instructors with OET knowledge.
The proposition depends on GCC and ECFMG route context.
Acceptance by ECFMG and other healthcare authorities is central to the value proposition.

scope_variance_risk.reason: The GCC audience is coherent, but destination-country rules and physician sub-specialties can vary.

expansion_variance_risk.reason: Moving into other countries or professions would change both acceptance rules and buyer expectations substantially.

subsidy_exists.reason: No subsidy or reimbursement route is central to this offer.

subsidy_eligibility_status.reason: The product is sold as a direct premium consumer offer.

subsidy_conflict_flag.reason: No funding claim is being made.

market_regulatory_context.reason: This product sits directly next to ECFMG certification and healthcare-regulator acceptance.

claim_language_risk.reason: ECFMG and route wording can quickly cross into formal acceptance claims if not kept narrow.

authority_dependency_risk.reason: The value proposition depends on formal route acceptance by ECFMG and healthcare authorities.

competitive_gap_risk.reason: Official free OET preparation resources already exist, so the premium offer must justify itself on physician specificity and feedback depth.

numeric_claim_risk.reason: The letter-count and cohort-format claims are internal and unverified.

subsidy_conflict_risk.reason: The card does not rely on a subsidy story.

positioning_risk.reason: The product is closer to a premium, route-sensitive service than to a simple exam-prep course.

claim_conflicts.claim_text: ECFMG now requires OET only and has dropped IELTS.
claim_conflicts.what_is_wrong: The reviewed sources confirm OET Medicine is approved and essential for Pathways, but they do not prove the broader 'OET only' claim.

claim_conflicts.claim_text: The curriculum includes 24 corrected medical letters and uses doctor-exclusive cohorts only.
claim_conflicts.what_is_wrong: These are internal design choices and not public facts.

recommended_manual_review.reason: Keep the ECFMG-related claim language narrow and source-tied.
Validate whether the premium doctor-only positioning is compelling enough against free OET materials.

sources.title: What is the OET Test, where and how do I take it
